user_outcome: Active and retired ownership intervals plus sync provenance preserve disjoint evidence while invalidating or blocking stale overlap.
rationale: A displayed PASS is not current integration evidence after sibling synchronization. C-08 reconstructs authoritative authored intervals and recognizes only Baton's exact synchronization topology so disjoint sibling bytes survive while overlapping reviewed evidence is invalidated deterministically.
in_scope: Reconstruct each slice's active authoritative interval from its immutable start_commit through the current pinned implementation_head, walking first-parent non-merge authored commits and validating every C-04 semantic path and C-05 report identity.
Retain terminal failed or re-sliced candidate intervals as retired ownership evidence without treating their historical PASS or former implementation_head as current readiness.

Recognize synchronization only as a validated two-parent merge whose parents, tree, authority refs, release-record conflict treatment, and first-parent position satisfy C-08; arbitrary merges, rebases, octopus merges, squash commits, and copied trees are not synchronization provenance.

Compare post-review synchronization contributions to active candidate paths and later authoritative intervals, preserving disjoint additions and deterministically invalidating the exact overlapping reviewed interval with its synchronization merge, parent-2 rollback baseline, and invalidated review head.
Fail closed on missing ancestry, rewritten refs, ambiguous ownership, overlap with a later authoritative interval, or any provenance shape that cannot support one deterministic disposition.
out_of_scope: Computing the canonical semantic manifest and fingerprint belongs to S07-canonical-semantic-scope.
Validating full report blobs and lifecycle phase order belongs to S08-report-ledger-identity and S09-lifecycle-fsm-adjudication.
Executing rollback or deciding overall integration readiness belongs to S12-rollback-readiness.
Performing a merge through a public adapter belongs to S15-unified-track-merge.

effort_complexity.rationale: First-parent history, synchronization topology, active and retired ownership, and post-review overlap interact across multiple immutable Git identities.

risks.description: A merge-only sibling contribution is attributed to a slice or silently invalidates unrelated verified work.
risks.mitigation: Derive authored intervals only from validated first-parent non-merge commits and compare the recognized synchronization contribution path-by-path against pinned C-04 candidate sets.

risks.description: Historical PASS evidence is reused as current readiness after its implementation head has been invalidated.
risks.mitigation: Model retired ownership separately from active authority; retired intervals participate in collision and rollback scope but never satisfy a current PASS predicate.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN a linear track contains two slices with valid C-05 authoritative reports and disjoint C-04 candidate sets separated by recognized release-wt synchronization merges THE SYSTEM SHALL reconstruct each slice's first-parent non-merge authored interval, retain both active authorities, and attribute no merge-only sibling path to either slice (C-04, C-05, C-08, N-06).

WHEN a recognized two-parent synchronization merge changes a path in the newest authoritative review_scope_head candidate set THE SYSTEM SHALL invalidate that active interval, clear no historical ledger entry, and return the exact invalidating_sync_merge, parent-2 rollback_baseline_commit, and invalidated_review_head required by C-08 (C-08, N-06, N-07).

WHILE an earlier slice is terminal re_slice_required and a later slice has a valid authoritative interval, THE SYSTEM SHALL retain the earlier complete candidate set as retired ownership, SHALL NOT count its former PASS as integration authority, and SHALL keep the later disjoint interval active (C-05, C-06, C-08, N-04, N-06).

IF a candidate sync is an octopus, squash, rebase, copied tree, parent-reversed merge, has an unretained or wrong authority parent, changes release records outside the permitted composition, or cannot resolve required ancestry THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject it as unrecognized provenance and SHALL leave every status and ref unchanged (C-03, C-08, N-06, N-09).

IF a post-sync invalidated candidate set overlaps any later authoritative slice candidate set THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL block automatic rollback classification with both slice IDs and the overlapping paths, require track reconstruction, and SHALL NOT narrow either ownership interval (C-08, N-06, N-07).
